architecture_fit.note: Attention-only 2L×4H, MLP removed, single documented delta (per-head rel-pos bias); minimal and solves the task (count=2), though the head count is partly engineered by seeding exactly two heads.

baseline_comparison.note: Two strawmen under identical measurement — untrained checkpoint counts 0, all-heads-seeded counts 8 — plus the benchmark's guess-4 baseline; clearly shows where the method fails.

faithfulness.note: Causal ablation on the model's own copy logits: removing both counted heads collapses accuracy to 0.004, removing all 6 distractors leaves it at 1.0 — direct evidence the counted heads are load-bearing.

operating_range.note: Length sweep 16→512 (>1.5 orders) holds 16→128 then honestly breaks at 256+ with an explained dilution argument; noise 1e-3→1e1 and 6-seed reseed both hold count=2.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Not a pure hand-built circuit, but the rel-pos bias is hand-seeded and the all-seeded strawman is a hand-set circuit yielding count 8, demonstrating mechanistic understanding of the offset knob.

visual_judgement.note: Per-head bar chart with the 0.5 line makes the count literally the bars above the line; ablation and operating-range panels are legible and on-point.

visualisation_rationale.note: README 'Why this visualisation' ties each panel to the claim — graded (~0.7) scores prove non-degenerate separation, ablation shows causality, range panel shows the failure edge.

notes: A strong trained-checkpoint attempt: minimal attention-only delta from base_model, perfect canonical count accuracy, and genuinely convincing causal ablation showing the two counted heads are load-bearing while distractors are dead weight. Operating-range and strawman coverage are thorough and honest about the 256+ failure edge. The main reservation is that the count is partly engineered by seeding exactly two heads, but the ablation and discriminating strawmen (0 and 8) substantially mitigate the circularity concern.
